D1 Optics L2 Understand Reference

Apparent Depth

slide a coin deeper in water and watch its apparent depth lag the real one

Rays bending at the water surface make a submerged object appear nearer the surface — its apparent depth is the real depth divided by n.

Slide the real depth d and the refractive index n. The dashed gold rays back-trace to the apparent coin position, always shallower than the real one.
